notes: FlashMLX and ollama use base model (Meta-Llama-3-8B-4bit). LM Studio and bodega use instruct variant (Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct-4bit) due to chat template requirements. ollama uses GGUF format; all others use MLX safetensors.

flashmlx.notes: N-step graph batching (eval_batch_size=16), mx.async_eval, concat KV cache

ollama.notes: llama.cpp Metal backend, granular timing from native API

bodega.model_format: Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct-4bit (MLX, shared from LM Studio path)

summary.analysis: At 8B scale, FlashMLX's graph batching optimization provides less benefit — the per-token compute dominates. LM Studio and bodega (both MLX-based) achieve ~68 tok/s decode, outperforming FlashMLX's 48 tok/s. FlashMLX's TTFT is also worst at 361ms vs ollama's 18ms. The N-step graph batching that won big at 0.6B adds graph construction overhead that hurts at larger model sizes.
